input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

samplesheet.description: The CSV file that specifies the various samples and metadata to feed into the pipeline

sample_base_dir.description: The directory where lookups for the files specified in the samplesheet will be made

reference_file.description: The reference that contains the gene or CDS of interest. Note that this file type depends on the pre-processing mode selected.

operating_modes.title: Operating Modes
operating_modes.description: Paramters that change the operating mode of the pipeline

aligner.description: What aligner to use for the main alignment

multi_timepoint_alignment.description: This run should produce timepoint-stacked alignments

build_phylogeny.description: Infer a phylogenetic tree (and, later, a per-site variation heatmap) from the computed alignment(s)

phylogeny_alignment_type.description: Which computed alignment(s) to feed into phylogenetic tree inference

phylogeny_baseline_method.description: How to choose the baseline sequence used for the (future) per-site variation heatmap

aga_preprocess_arguments.title: AGA Preprocess Arguments
aga_preprocess_arguments.description: Arguments altering how AGA functions when using it as the preprocessing strategy

region_of_interest.description: The name of the CDS or protein that is to be extracted from the supplied files. Still required if not using AGA as a pre-processing step

region_shorthand.description: What to shorten the region name to

aga_args.description: If using the AGA preprocessing, what arguments to supply to AGA

minimap.title: Minimap
minimap.description: Arguments specifying how minimap2 must trim sequences

filtering_options.title: Filtering Options
filtering_options.description: Options passed onto the functional filter

ff_max_stop_pct.description: The maximum percentage along the read that a stop codon can find itself

ff_include_no_stop_codons.description: Allow sequences that do not contain any stop codons to be considered functional

ff_include_frameshifts.description: Include sequences that potentially contain a frameshift to be considered functional

ff_acceptable_pct_loss.description: An acceptable proportion (0-1) of the median protein length that can be lost before a sequence is considered non-functional

ff_expected_length.description: The expected length of the protein. If left blank, this is set to the median length of the sequences in the input.

use_kmer_filtering.description: Turns on/off the kmer filtering step.

match_kmers_start.description: What kmer(s) to match at the start. Comma-separated if multiple.

match_kmers_end.description: What kmer(s) to match at the end. Comma-separated if multiple.

length_based_filtering_options.title: Length Based Filtering Options
length_based_filtering_options.description: Options controlling the length-based functional filter (used when functional_filter_method is LENGTH_BASED_FILTERING)

lbf_threshold_mode.description: How the minimum-length threshold is determined for length-based filtering

lbf_min_length.description: Minimum sequence length to keep when lbf_threshold_mode is FIXED. Required (and only used) in that mode.

lbf_min_tolerance.description: How much shorter than the center length a sequence may be and still be kept. An absolute base count (e.g. '20') or a percentage of the center (e.g. '20%'). Defaults to 0 (the center acts as a strict minimum) if neither this nor lbf_tolerance is set. Cannot be combined with lbf_tolerance.

lbf_max_tolerance.description: How much longer than the center length a sequence may be and still be kept. An absolute base count or a percentage of the center. If neither this nor lbf_tolerance is set, there is no upper bound. Cannot be combined with lbf_tolerance.

lbf_tolerance.description: Symmetric tolerance: shorthand for setting both lbf_min_tolerance and lbf_max_tolerance to the same value. Cannot be combined with lbf_min_tolerance/lbf_max_tolerance.

miscellaneous_process_arguments.title: Miscellaneous Process Arguments
miscellaneous_process_arguments.description: Arguments that can be supplied to the various third-party tools in the pipeline

mafft_fast_align_args.description: When using the custom pre-processing mode, what args to supply to MAFFT

mafft_args.description: Arguments to supply to MAFFT if using it as the aligner

iqtree_args.description: If building trees, what args to supply to IQTREE

tcoffee_regressive_args.description: Parameters to pass to TCoffee Regressive.

clustalo_args.description: Parameters to pass to Clustal Omega

max_memory.description: The maximum amount of memory to be allocated to a process

max_cpus.description: The maximum number of CPU cores that can be allocated to a process

max_time.description: The maximum amount of time that a process can be allowed to run for until it is killed

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

run_name.description: A  human-readable identifier for this run

slurm_queue.description: If running on an HPC with slurm, what queue to use

publish_dir_mode.description: The mode to handle output files. It is recommended not to touch this unless you know what you are doing

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.
